item.title: The weak shouldn't have to fear the powerful, and the powerful shouldn't simply be allowed to take whatever they want without consequence. Power should be a burden, not a shield.
For those of you who read the trigger warnings and said, "Accidental cannibalism?! Count me in!" This one's for you.
My one fear is that tomorrow I may die without having come to know myself.
They had only ever discussed books but what, in this life, is more personal than books?
item.description: "The Storied Life of A.J. Fikry" by Gabrielle Zevin

item.title: Do not ask for whom the bell tolls. Ask who would mourn in hearing it. If answer comes there none then more power to you (especially if you are planning an electrocution).
An animal may be ferocious and cunning enough, but it takes a real man to tell a lie.
item.description: "The Island of Dr. Moreau" by H.G. Wells

item.title: To everyone with religious trauma. I hope this helps heal you in some small, very weird way. Also to Andrew Scott. This is all your fault.
I don't believe there has ever been a more terrible weapon in this world than the word they.
item.description: "Sky Full of Elephants" by Cebo Campbell

item.title: Amanda, You look at life through the eyes of a child, and I don't know where you got them, and it's making me very uncomfortable. Please stop.
You should try not to talk so much, friend. You'll sound far less stupid that way.
item.description: "Mistborn: The Final Empire" by Brando Sando

item.title: I must confess that I lost faith in the sanity of the world.
item.description: "The Island of Dr. Moreau" by H.G. Wells

item.title: It's good for a person to be terrorized by a goat. Hard to get high and mighty when there's something chasing you for vegetables
Ugh! How many stories about love, copulation, marriage and death already exist, not one of which tells the truth! How sick I am of well-constructed plots and brilliant writing!
It sounds plausible enough tonight, but wait until tomorrow. Wait for the common sense of the morning.
Finite does not mean much if you can't tell any practical difference between it and infinite.
item.description: "A Short Stay in Hell" by Steven L. Peck
